properties.label: Topics
properties.description: The Kafka topic names to consume from (comma-separated list or pattern based on Topic Format)

properties.label: Topic Format
properties.description: Specifies whether the Topic Name(s) provided are a comma-separated list of names or a single regular expression pattern

properties.label: Kafka Connection Service
properties.description: Specifies the Kafka Connection Service to use for connecting to Kafka brokers

properties.label: Group ID
properties.description: The Kafka Consumer Group ID to use

properties.label: Commit Offsets
properties.description: Specifies whether to commit offsets to Kafka after processing

properties.label: Max Uncommitted Size
properties.description: Maximum number of FlowFiles to be processed without committing offsets

properties.label: Max Uncommitted Time
properties.description: Maximum time to wait before committing offsets

properties.label: Processing Strategy
properties.description: Strategy for processing Kafka messages

properties.label: Output Strategy
properties.description: Strategy for outputting Kafka messages when using Record processing

properties.label: Separate By Key
properties.description: If true, FlowFiles will be created for messages with unique message keys

properties.label: Key Format
properties.description: Format of the Kafka message key

properties.label: Key Record Reader
properties.description: Record reader for parsing message keys when Key Format is 'record'

properties.label: Record Reader
properties.description: Record reader for parsing message values when Processing Strategy is 'RECORD'

properties.label: Record Writer
properties.description: Record writer for output when Processing Strategy is 'RECORD'

properties.label: Key Attribute Encoding

options.label: Do Not Add Key as Attribute

properties.description: Specifies how to encode the message key to the FlowFile attribute

properties.label: Header Encoding
properties.description: Encoding for Kafka message headers

properties.label: Header Name Pattern
properties.description: Regular expression pattern for Kafka headers to add as FlowFile attributes

properties.label: Header Name Prefix
properties.description: Prefix to add to header names when adding as FlowFile attributes

properties.label: Message Demarcator
properties.description: Specifies the string that should be used to separate messages when multiple messages are written to a single FlowFile

properties.label: Auto Offset Reset
properties.description: What to do when there is no initial offset in Kafka or if the current offset does not exist

properties.label: Kafka Connection Service
properties.description: Provides connections to Kafka Broker for publishing Kafka Records

properties.label: Topic Name
properties.description: Name of the Kafka Topic to which the Processor publishes Kafka Records

properties.label: Failure Strategy
properties.description: Specifies how the processor handles a FlowFile if it is unable to publish the data to Kafka

properties.label: Delivery Guarantee
properties.description: Specifies the requirement for guaranteeing that a message is sent to Kafka. Corresponds to Kafka Client acks property.

properties.label: Compression Type
properties.description: Specifies the compression strategy for records sent to Kafka. Corresponds to Kafka Client compression.type property.

properties.label: Max Request Size
properties.description: The maximum size of a request in bytes. Corresponds to Kafka Client max.request.size property.

properties.label: Transactions Enabled
properties.description: Specifies whether to provide transactional guarantees when communicating with Kafka.

properties.label: Transactional ID Prefix
properties.description: Specifies the KafkaProducer config transactional.id will be a generated UUID and will be prefixed with the configured string.

properties.label: Partitioner Class
properties.description: Specifies which class to use to compute a partition id for a message. Corresponds to Kafka Client partitioner.class property.

properties.label: Partition
properties.description: Specifies the Kafka Partition destination for Records.

properties.label: Message Demarcator
properties.description: Specifies the string to use for demarcating multiple messages within a single FlowFile.

properties.label: Record Reader
properties.description: The Record Reader to use for incoming FlowFiles

properties.label: Record Writer
properties.description: The Record Writer to use in order to serialize the data before sending to Kafka

properties.label: Publish Strategy
properties.description: The format used to publish the incoming FlowFile record to Kafka.

properties.label: Message Key Field
properties.description: The name of a field in the Input Records that should be used as the Key for the Kafka message.

properties.label: FlowFile Attribute Header Pattern
properties.description: A Regular Expression that is matched against all FlowFile attribute names. Any attribute whose name matches the pattern will be added to the Kafka messages as a Header.

properties.label: Header Encoding
properties.description: For any attribute that is added as a Kafka Record Header, this property indicates the Character Encoding to use for serializing the headers.

properties.label: Kafka Key
properties.description: The Key to use for the Message. If not specified, the FlowFile attribute 'kafka.key' is used as the message key, if it is present.

properties.label: Kafka Key Attribute Encoding

options.label: Do Not Add Key as Attribute

properties.description: FlowFiles that are emitted have an attribute named 'kafka.key'. This property dictates how the value of the attribute should be encoded.

properties.label: Record Key Writer
properties.description: The Record Key Writer to use for outgoing FlowFiles

properties.label: Record Metadata Strategy
properties.description: Specifies whether the Record's metadata (topic and partition) should come from the Record's metadata field or if it should come from the configured Topic Name and Partition / Partitioner class properties

properties.label: Broker URI
properties.description: The URI(s) to use to connect to the MQTT broker

properties.label: File Size
properties.description: The size of the file that will be used

properties.label: Batch Size
properties.description: The number of FlowFiles to be transferred in each invocation

properties.label: Data Format
properties.description: Specifies whether the data should be Text or Binary

properties.label: Unique FlowFiles
properties.description: If true, each FlowFile that is generated will be unique.

properties.label: Custom Text
properties.description: Custom text to be used as content of the generated FlowFiles

properties.label: Character Set
properties.description: Specifies the character set to use when writing the bytes of Custom Text

properties.label: Mime Type
properties.description: Specifies the value to set for the "mime.type" attribute

properties.label: Script File
properties.description: Path to script file to execute. Only one of Script File or Script Body may be used

properties.label: Script Body
properties.description: Body of script to execute. Only one of Script File or Script Body may be used

properties.label: Failure Strategy
properties.description: What to do with unhandled exceptions.

properties.label: Additional Classpath
properties.description: Classpath list separated by semicolon or comma.

properties.label: Script File
properties.description: Path to script file to execute. Only one of Script File or Script Body may be used

properties.label: Script Body
properties.description: Body of script to execute. Only one of Script File or Script Body may be used

properties.label: Failure Strategy
properties.description: What to do with unhandled exceptions.

properties.label: Additional Classpath
properties.description: Classpath list separated by semicolon or comma.

properties.label: Record Reader
properties.description: Specifies the Controller Service to use for reading incoming data

properties.label: Record Writer
properties.description: Specifies the Controller Service to use for writing out the records

properties.label: Include Zero Record FlowFiles
properties.description: When converting an incoming FlowFile, if the conversion results in no data, this property specifies whether or not a FlowFile will be sent to the corresponding relationship

properties.label: Destination
properties.description: Indicates whether the results of the JsonPath evaluation are written to the FlowFile content or a FlowFile attribute; if using attribute, must specify the Attribute Name property. If set to flowfile-content, only one JsonPath may be specified, and the property name is ignored.

properties.label: Return Type
properties.description: Indicates the desired return type of the JSON Path expressions. Selecting 'auto-detect' will set the return type to 'json' for a Destination of 'flowfile-content', and 'scalar' for a Destination of 'flowfile-attribute'.

properties.label: Path Not Found Behavior
properties.description: Indicates how to handle missing JSON path expressions when destination is set to 'flowfile-attribute'. Selecting 'warn' will generate a warning when a JSON path expression is not found. Selecting 'skip' will omit attributes for any unmatched JSON path expressions.

properties.label: Null Value Representation
properties.description: Indicates the desired representation of JSON Path expressions resulting in a null value.

properties.label: Max String Length
properties.description: The maximum allowed length of a string value when parsing the JSON document

properties.label: Log Level
properties.description: The Log Level to use when logging the Attributes

properties.label: Log Payload
properties.description: If true, the FlowFile's payload will be logged, in addition to its attributes; otherwise, just the Attributes will be logged.

properties.label: Attributes to Log
properties.description: A comma-separated list of Attributes to Log. If not specified, all attributes will be logged unless `Attributes to Log by Regular Expression` is modified. There's an AND relationship between the two properties.

properties.label: Attributes to Log Regular Expression
properties.description: A regular expression indicating the Attributes to Log. If not specified, all attributes will be logged unless `Attributes to Log` is modified. There's an AND relationship between the two properties.

properties.label: Attributes to Ignore
properties.description: A comma-separated list of Attributes to ignore. If not specified, no attributes will be ignored unless `Attributes to Ignore by Regular Expression` is modified. There's an OR relationship between the two properties.

properties.label: Attributes to Ignore Regular Expression
properties.description: A regular expression indicating the Attributes to Ignore. If not specified, no attributes will be ignored unless `Attributes to Ignore` is modified. There's an OR relationship between the two properties.

properties.label: Log FlowFile Properties
properties.description: Specifies whether or not to log FlowFile "properties", such as Entry Date, Lineage Start Date, and content size

properties.label: Output Format
properties.description: Specifies the format to use for logging FlowFile attributes

properties.label: Log Prefix
properties.description: Log prefix appended to the log lines. It helps to distinguish the output of multiple LogAttribute processors.

properties.label: Character Set
properties.description: The name of the Character Set to use

properties.label: Hadoop Configuration Resources
properties.description: A file or comma separated list of files which contains the Hadoop file system configuration. Without this, Hadoop will search the classpath for a 'core-site.xml' and 'hdfs-site.xml' file or will revert to a default configuration. To use swebhdfs, see 'Additional Details' section of PutHDFS's documentation.

properties.label: Kerberos User Service
properties.description: Specifies the Kerberos User Controller Service that should be used for authenticating with Kerberos

properties.label: Additional Classpath Resources
properties.description: A comma-separated list of paths to files and/or directories that will be added to the classpath and used for loading native libraries. When specifying a directory, all files with in the directory will be added to the classpath, but further sub-directories will not be included.

properties.label: Record Reader
properties.description: The service for reading records from incoming flow files.

properties.label: Directory
properties.description: The parent directory to which files should be written. Will be created if it doesn't exist.

properties.label: Compression Type
properties.description: The type of compression for the file being written.

properties.label: Overwrite Files
properties.description: Whether or not to overwrite existing files in the same directory with the same name. When set to false, flow files will be routed to failure when a file exists in the same directory with the same name.

properties.label: Permissions Umask
properties.description: A umask represented as an octal number which determines the permissions of files written to HDFS. This overrides the Hadoop Configuration dfs.umaskmode

properties.label: Remote Group
properties.description: Changes the group of the HDFS file to this value after it is written. This only works if NiFi is running as a user that has HDFS super user privilege to change group

properties.label: Remote Owner
properties.description: Changes the owner of the HDFS file to this value after it is written. This only works if NiFi is running as a user that has HDFS super user privilege to change owner

properties.label: Row Group Size
properties.description: The row group size used by the Parquet writer. The value is specified in the format of <Data Size> <Data Unit> where Data Unit is one of B, KB, MB, GB, TB.

properties.label: Page Size
properties.description: The page size used by the Parquet writer. The value is specified in the format of <Data Size> <Data Unit> where Data Unit is one of B, KB, MB, GB, TB.

properties.label: Dictionary Page Size
properties.description: The dictionary page size used by the Parquet writer. The value is specified in the format of <Data Size> <Data Unit> where Data Unit is one of B, KB, MB, GB, TB.

properties.label: Max Padding Size
properties.description: The maximum amount of padding that will be used to align row groups with blocks in the underlying filesystem. If the underlying filesystem is not a block filesystem like HDFS, this has no effect. The value is specified in the format of <Data Size> <Data Unit> where Data Unit is one of B, KB, MB, GB, TB.

properties.label: Enable Dictionary Encoding
properties.description: Specifies whether dictionary encoding should be enabled for the Parquet writer

properties.label: Enable Validation
properties.description: Specifies whether validation should be enabled for the Parquet writer

properties.label: Writer Version
properties.description: Specifies the version used by Parquet writer

properties.label: Avro Write Old List Structure
properties.description: Specifies the value for 'parquet.avro.write-old-list-structure' in the underlying Parquet library

properties.label: Avro Add List Element Records
properties.description: Specifies the value for 'parquet.avro.add-list-element-records' in the underlying Parquet library

properties.label: Remove CRC Files
properties.description: Specifies whether the corresponding CRC file should be deleted upon successfully writing a Parquet file
